name: Bermejales Reservoir Perimeter Trail
name_original: Embalse de Bermejales — circular por Fornes y Arenas del Rey
description: Cycle a moderate 16.1-mile (25.9 km) loop around Embalse de Bermejales, enjoying blue waters, olive groves, and mountain views.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Embalse de los Bermejales offers routes through a landscape characterized by striking turquoise waters, surrounded by the mountains of the Sierra de Almijara. The region features diverse Mediterranean vegetation, including pine, olive, eucalyptus, almond, and cherry trees, providing both visual appeal and shade. Cyclists can explore varied terrain, from relatively mellow lakeside trails to more challenging routes with significant elevation gains in the surrounding national parks. Downstream, the Cacín river flows through an impressive gorge with large rock walls, adding to the scenic diversity. Best touring cycling routes around Embalse de los Bermejales The most popular touring cycling route is Bermejales Reservoir Perimeter Trail, a 16.1 miles (25.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 44 minutes to complete. This moderate route circles the lake, offering views of the blue waters and olive groves. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is View of Alhama de Granada loop from Arenas del Rey, a difficult 20.7 miles (33.4 km) path. This route provides panoramic views of the historic town of Alhama de Granada and the surrounding mountainous terrain. Local touring cyclists also love the Alto de los Morros Viewpoint – La Resinera loop from Fornes, a 27.0 miles (43.5 km) trail leading through varied landscapes with significant elevation, often completed in about 4 hours 4 minutes. Touring cycling around Embalse de los Bermejales is defined by turquoise waters, mountain backdrops, and Mediterranean vegetation. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate lakeside loops to more challenging climbs. The routes in Embalse de los Bermejales are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Embalse de los Bermejales's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Embalse de los Bermejales?
A: There are over 25 touring cycling routes available around Embalse de los Bermejales. These routes offer a variety of experiences, from moderate lakeside loops to more challenging climbs through mountainous terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Embalse de los Bermejales?
A: The region around Embalse de los Bermejales is ideal for touring cycling during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. The Mediterranean climate ensures pleasant conditions, and the surrounding vegetation, including pine, olive, and almond trees, provides both visual appeal and welcome shade.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of touring cycling routes in the area?
A: The routes around Embalse de los Bermejales cater to various skill levels. While there is 1 easy route, you'll find 3 moderate routes, and a significant number of challenging options, with 24 difficult routes featuring considerable elevation gains. This diversity allows cyclists to choose paths that match their fitness and experience.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Embalse de los Bermejales?
A: Yes, a major draw for touring cyclists is the trail that circles the lake. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046231178/bermejales-reservoir-perimeter-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bermejales Reservoir Perimeter Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate loop, offering stunning views of the blue waters and olive groves. Other circular options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32064901" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Bermejales – Los Bermejales Reservoir loop from Pantano de los Bermejales&lt;/a&gt; also provide extensive exploration.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while cycling around Embalse de los Bermejales?
A: Cyclists can enjoy the striking turquoise waters of the reservoir itself, backed by the Sierra de Almijara mountains. The area is rich in Mediterranean vegetation, and downstream from the dam, the Cacín river flows through an impressive gorge. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/239989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Bermejales Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5152301" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de los Bermejales&lt;/a&gt; itself.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints worth visiting on a touring cycling trip?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5427076" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de las Yeseras&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views of the Embalse de los Bermejales, providing excellent photo opportunities. Additionally, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31526596" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Alhama de Granada loop from Arenas del Rey&lt;/a&gt; provide sweeping vistas of the historic town and surrounding mountains.

Q: Can I explore any historical sites or charming villages during my ride?
A: Yes, several small, traditional Spanish villages like Arenas del Rey, Alhama de Granada, and Jayena are situated around the reservoir, offering a chance to experience local culture. Alhama de Granada is known for its historic hot sulfur baths. You can also visit the important archaeological site of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3159541" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de los Bermejales&lt;/a&gt;, a prehistoric grave that was relocated and rebuilt.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes suitable for beginners?
A: While many routes are more challenging, there is 1 easy route available, and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046231178/bermejales-reservoir-perimeter-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bermejales Reservoir Perimeter Trail&lt;/a&gt; is mostly paved and suitable for various fitness levels, making it a good option for families or those new to touring cycling.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes around Embalse de los Bermejales?
A: Parking is generally available at various points around the reservoir and in the nearby villages. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3305216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Five-Branch Pine&lt;/a&gt; is listed as a parking highlight. It's advisable to check specific starting points for routes for designated parking areas.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the charming villages surrounding the reservoir, such as Bermejales, Arenas del Rey, and Alhama de Granada, offer opportunities for refreshments and accommodation. You'll find local cafes and restaurants to refuel after your ride.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes here?
A: The terrain is quite diverse. You can expect relatively mellow, mostly paved trails around the lake, but also more challenging routes with significant elevation gains as you venture into the surrounding mountainous areas and national parks. The region features a mix of Mediterranean vegetation, including olive groves and pine forests.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Embalse de los Bermejales?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the stunning turquoise waters, the picturesque mountain backdrops, and the diverse Mediterranean vegetation that provides both visual appeal and shade. The variety of routes, from lakeside loops to challenging climbs, is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced riders will find plenty of challenging options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38221183" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de los Morros Viewpoint – La Resinera loop from Fornes&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 27-mile (43.5 km) trail with significant elevation. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32083954" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mesón Los Prados – Shelter hut loop from Arenas del Rey&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 60 km with substantial climbing.
tips: Admittedly, reservoirs are not uncommon in this area - they are often the only way to survive the drought months during the summer. Nevertheless, each is a unique highlight in itself - and not only in terms of landscape but also in terms of color. This one is permeated with such a pure light blue, as if someone had dissolved several tons of glacier ice candy in it.
Los Bermejales Reservoir, a dam located in the Arenas del Rey, formes and Jayena, a good bathing area for the summer months and also this reservoir is possible to navigate through it with a canoe

It is located in the municipality of Arenas del Rey and is fed by the rivers and streams of the Sierra de Almijara mountains. The reservoir was completed in 1958 and has a capacity of approximately 104 million cubic meters of water. 
With beautiful views of the natural surroundings.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This touring bicycle route around the Embalse de Bermejales is approximately 25.9 kilometers long and generally takes about 1 hour and 44 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is primarily composed of paved surfaces, making it well-suited for touring bicycles. While it's a moderate ride with an elevation gain of around 430 meters, the smooth surfaces contribute to a pleasant cycling experience.

faqs.question: What scenic highlights can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll be treated to beautiful views of the natural surroundings, including the View of the Los Bermejales reservoir and the Los Bermejales Reservoir itself, known for its beautiful blue waters. The route also features a Quiet road between olive trees, offering a blend of Mediterranean vegetation and mountain landscapes, with the Sierra Tejeda y Almijara mountains providing a stunning backdrop.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region is particularly striking at sunrise and sunset. Given the warm climate of Andalusia, spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable temperatures for cycling. Summer can be very hot, so early mornings or late afternoons would be preferable if visiting during those months.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Embalse de Bermejales circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, the route circulates through the municipalities of Fornes and Arenas del Rey. You would typically find parking available within or near these towns, which provide access points to the reservoir area.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route itself is on paved surfaces around the reservoir, specific regulations for dogs on the cycling path are not provided. However, as it's within the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash and ensure they are well-behaved, especially around wildlife and other visitors.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle this route in the Natural Park?
faqs.answer: This route is located within the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public routes within the park. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning nature conservation and waste disposal.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers a significant portion of the Sendero perimetral del Pantano de los Bermejales (48%) and also briefly touches sections of the Transandalus (2%), Tajos de los Bermejales (2%), Andalucía - South Route (Provincia de Granada) (2%), Camino de la Cuesta del Algar (4%), Camino de Napoleón (2%), and Camino Romero (2%).

faqs.question: Is this a popular or more secluded cycling route?
faqs.answer: The region surrounding the Embalse de los Bermejales is often described as a 'hidden gem,' offering a tranquil and beautiful escape from more crowded tourist destinations. This suggests it's a more secluded option, perfect for those seeking peace and natural beauty.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through the municipalities of Fornes and Arenas del Rey. These towns typically offer local amenities such as cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options, providing convenient stops for refreshments or overnight stays.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty and approximately 26 km length, this route might be challenging for very young children or those new to cycling. However, for families with older children who are experienced cyclists, the paved terrain and beautiful scenery could make for an enjoyable outing. Consider the total distance and elevation gain when planning with children.

waypoints.name: View of the Los Bermejales reservoir
